title: Analytical Pit-Window Economics Features
summary: Strategy modeling references simple but useful analytical relationships: stint length trades off degradation against pit-loss, and undercut success depends on old-tyre drop versus new-tyre warmup.
rationale: Strategy modeling references simple but useful analytical relationships: stint length trades off degradation against pit-loss, and undercut success depends on old-tyre drop versus new-tyre warmup. Those economics are not explicitly encoded in the current baseline.
implementation_hint: Derive first-order decision features from observed degradation slope and estimated laps remaining: approximate stay-out cost over the next 1-3 laps, implied optimal stint length proxy, finish-on-current-tyre feasibility, and undercut benefit proxy comparing current degradation to expected post-pit reset on a candidate compound. Make these race- and compound-conditional but target-free.
expected_effect: Adds decision-theoretic structure that should especially help late-stint calls and distinguish routine tyre aging from economically justified pit windows.
risk: Pit-loss and fresh-tyre gain are unobserved in this dataset, so proxies may be crude; too many formula-based features can collapse into noisy duplicates of TyreLife and LapTime_Delta.
